assertions.reason: A real SIGTERM harness launched server/dist/index.js on ports 9510/9511, attached a mocked Feishu user through BotService, observed the attached-user shutdown message, confirmed the server exited cleanly with code 0, and verified the wrapper continued local stdio proxying by echoing a post-shutdown local prompt with no console or unhandled errors.

commandsRun.observation: The dedicated harness sent SIGTERM to the real relay server process, recorded server exit code 0 with stderr 'Received SIGTERM, shutting down relay server.', observed the attached-user message 'Relay server is unavailable; detached from [workspace-shutdown-sigterm-1775094612070-7e525c]. Local proxy will continue until the relay reconnects.', and confirmed the wrapper still accepted and echoed a local turn/start prompt after relay shutdown.
Server suite passed 25/25 tests, including relay-server shutdown coverage ('notifies wrappers before shutting down').
Bot suite passed 49/49 tests, including the integration case 'keeps the local proxy alive and informs attached users when the relay shuts down'.

summary: Validated VAL-CROSS-020 as passing through a real SIGTERM shutdown harness plus supporting server/bot suites: the bot notified the attached user, the wrapper stayed alive in degraded local-proxy mode, and no crashes or unhandled errors were observed.
